description.value:
A greatsword whose fuller is etched like a musical staff. Each kill rings clear in the steel. The edge never dulls.

Curse. After the blade tastes enough blood, the wielder begins to lose their voice: first whispers fail, then speech in combat, then all words above a rasp. Only a greater restoration (or ending the curse) restores what was taken.

unidentified.description: A magnificent greatsword with staff-like etchings along the fuller. It hums faintly when swung.

cursedMeta.decoyAppearance: A +1 greatsword etched like a musical score; the steel sings on a killing stroke.
cursedMeta.trueNature: After enough killing blows the wielder loses their voice in stages until they cannot speak clearly; requires curse removal or greater restoration to undo.

curse.name: The Mournchord
curse.description: When the latent threshold is reached, the wielder loses speech in stages (whispers, then battle cries, then most words) as kills are tallied. Greater restoration can restore the voice until the curse is broken.

hints: Victory toasts die in the wielder's throat while the blade still rings.

escalation.effect: Hoarse mornings after fights; blamed on weather.
Words fail in initiative order; allies mishear orders.
